evals.name: create-new-monorepo-agents-md
evals.prompt: Create an AGENTS.md file for this Turborepo monorepo. It has apps/web (Next.js frontend), apps/api (Express backend), and packages/ui (shared components). The project uses pnpm workspaces.
evals.expected_output:
A well-structured AGENTS.md with:
- Project description
- Essential commands table
- Monorepo structure table with actual package names detected
- Progressive disclosure with reference index entries
- No hardcoded @repo/api or @repo/web — uses detected names
- Proper multi-entry indexing (start/middle/end)

evals.name: optimize-bloated-agents-md
evals.prompt: This AGENTS.md is 2000+ lines and includes detailed build steps, extensive debugging guides, and historical decisions. Optimize it using progressive disclosure.
evals.expected_output:
Optimized AGENTS.md that:
- Moves detailed SOPs to docs/references/
- Keeps only high-frequency info in Level 1
- Has trigger conditions for all references
- Includes multi-entry reference tables
- Has information recording principles section
- No mention of line counts or percentages

evals.name: detect-project-structure
evals.prompt: Analyze this project and determine if it's a monorepo or polyrepo. Show your detection evidence and confidence level.
evals.expected_output:
Analysis that shows:
- Checks for apps/, packages/, services/ directories
- Checks for workspace configs (pnpm-workspace.yaml, etc.)
- Checks for monorepo build systems (turbo.json, nx.json, lerna.json)
- Provides confidence level (High/Medium/Low)
- Lists detected packages with their actual names
- Asks for user confirmation if confidence is not High

evals.name: flexible-package-naming
evals.prompt: Create an AGENTS.md for a monorepo where the backend is called 'apps/backend' (not @repo/api) and there's also 'apps/admin' for the admin dashboard.
evals.expected_output:
AGENTS.md that:
- Uses actual detected package names (apps/backend, apps/admin)
- Does NOT assume @repo/api or @repo/web naming
- Shows flexible package name detection
- Has correct commands for the actual package names

evals.name: progressive-disclosure-references
evals.prompt: I'm adding a new feature that requires database migration. The current AGENTS.md doesn't have database info. Add it appropriately using progressive disclosure.
evals.expected_output:
Addition that:
- Assesses if database info is high-frequency (likely yes for dev)
- If high-frequency: adds essential info to Level 1
- Creates detailed migration guide in docs/references/migration.md
- Adds proper trigger condition to Level 1
- Includes content summary in the reference
